snippet.title: Ed Bastian, Delta CEO: Making Travel Magical
snippet.description:
Delta Air Lines is looking to revive the glory days of travel with a premium experience in the sky. Under Ed Bastian, who took over as CEO in 2016, Delta has improved the customer experience and grown revenue, while also navigating the operational challenges microenvironment that comes with running any airline. The result has been double-digit operating margins – a benchmark for major U.S. carriers.

In the latest episode of Exceptional Leaders, Freight Transportation & Airlines analyst Ravi Shanker sits down with Bastian to talk about everything from his advice on making hard decisions, to how it's possible to bring back the golden era of travel while keeping it accessible. https://mgstn.ly/3SDmwRr

snippet.title: Delta Airlines Stock Is Cheap
snippet.description:
Delta Airlines stock analysis. 
Discover under-the-radar stock ideas: https://www.overlookedalpha.com

The travel industry is booming right now. Airline passenger numbers have returned to pre-pandemic levels.  Last week, Delta Airlines reported record revenues of 15.6 billion for the quarter and the stock is up 42% year to date. 

At the current share price, Delta has a market cap of 30 billion. It’s got 8.5 billion of cash and investments and 18 billion of debt. So the enterprise value is just under 40 billion. 

Revenue over the last 12 months is 55.8 billion which is almost 20% above the 2019 peak. 
Net income, however, at 3 billion is still 37% below the peak. One reason is that Delta had to take on a lot of debt during the pandemic and that is costing the company almost a billion dollars a year in interest payments. 

But Delta is optimistic it can pay the debt off over time. And management gave an encouraging outlook. It thinks revenue will grow between 17-20% this year with an operating margin above 12%.  Free cash flow is expected to cruise above 4 billion in 2024. That means Delta stock is now valued at only 8 times next years free cash flow.

A key part of the investment case for Delta is its diversified revenue streams in particular its SkyMiles partnership with American Express. In an interview on CNBC, JP Morgan analyst Jamie Baker said that close to 1% of US GDP is now being charged on co-branded Delta American Express credit cards. This is a lucrative venture for Delta and could contribute almost 7 billion in revenue this year. That’s 63% growth since 2019.

#deltaairlinesstock #stockstobuy #stocks #overlookedalpha

snippet.title: Next month, according to Delta, many US flights will provide complimentary Wi-Fi.
snippet.description:
(AP) LAS VEGAS — According to a statement made by CEO Ed Bastian on Thursday at the CES technology trade show, starting in February, Delta Air Lines will offer free Wi-Fi on the majority of its domestic flights.

According to Bastian, T-Mobile will install high-speed, satellite-based broadband service on more than 700 airplanes by the end of the year. By the end of 2024, it intends to add complimentary Wi-Fi to international and Delta Connection flights. The service will make use of Viasat's hardware, a satellite internet provider based in the United States.

"It's always struck me that we fly to connect," Bastian said in an interview with The Associated Press. But we disengage while we're in the air.

#Celebrities #News #CelebrityNews

People want to be connected, thus the inability of airlines to connect in the air is something that Bastian believed needed to be addressed. He added that the pandemic-related shutdowns in 2020, when the majority of the airline's aircraft were grounded, hastened the implementation of the $1 billion project.


To utilize the Wi-Fi service, customers must have a SkyMiles frequent flyer account, which they may sign up for for free. The amount of passengers' gadgets that can connect to the inflight Wi-Fi is unrestricted. Beginning on February 1st, the complimentary service will be available on more than 500 aircraft.

snippet.title: Do You Walk the Talk?
snippet.description:
Do You Walk the Talk?

It's easy to assert your values on your company website.

But when the going gets tough, that's when integrity is called into question.

In this clip, Matt Burns and I discuss the steps Delta Air Lines took during COVID to demonstrate their values, even while taking a huge loss in revenue.

Watch the whole interview here, and make sure to subscribe!
